title: Java Multi Catch Custom Exception
description: Practice custom checked exceptions and multi-catch handlers.
learningGoals: Recall that a checked custom exception extends Exception and must be handled or declared.
Recognize that a multi-catch parameter is implicitly final.

completionChecks.prompt: What does the catch block print when input is "abc"?
choices: Nothing, because abc is a valid score
completionChecks.explanation: "abc" is not blank, so parseScore reaches Integer.parseInt("abc"), which throws NumberFormatException; the multi-catch handles it and prints its simple name.

completionChecks.prompt: What is true about e in catch (NumberFormatException | MissingScoreException e)?
choices: It can be reassigned to either exception type
It is implicitly final, so it cannot be reassigned in the catch block
It always has the type RuntimeException
It must be declared with var
completionChecks.explanation: A multi-catch parameter is implicitly final, so it cannot be reassigned; its static type is the common supertype of the listed exceptions.

completionChecks.prompt: What does the catch block print when input is an empty String?
choices: Nothing, because empty input is parsed as 0
completionChecks.explanation: parseScore checks text.trim().isEmpty() before parseInt, so an empty String throws the custom MissingScoreException.
